cot_content:
Okay, so I need to figure out how to tell my right and left earbuds apart without looking. The options are A or B. Let me think.

The question is about tying a knot in one of the earbuds so you can tell left from right by touch. The idea is that when you're using earbuds, you usually have one in each ear. If you have a knot on one side, you can feel which one is which without looking.

Option A says to tie a small knot at the speaker end of the left one. The speaker end is the part that goes in your ear, right? If you tie a knot there, maybe it would be a bit bulkier, so when you put it in your left ear, you can feel the knot. But wait, if the knot is on the speaker end, maybe it would be uncomfortable or change the fit. Also, when you're using the earbuds, the cord usually runs from the ear to the device. So the speaker end is the part that's in the ear.

Option B says to tie a knot at the connecting end of the left one. The connecting end is the part that connects to the device or the other earbud. If you tie a knot there, then when you hold the earbuds, you can feel the knot on the left side's connecting end. But if you're wearing them, you probably won't be able to feel the connecting end because that's on the outside, not in your ear.

So the better approach would be to tie the knot on the part that you can feel when it's in your ear. That would be the speaker end, so you can feel the knot in your left ear. Therefore, option A makes sense. But wait, some people might say that the knot on the connecting end (option B) is easier to identify when you're not wearing them, but the question specifically says "without looking" while using them. So if you're wearing them, you need to feel the difference. So the knot should be on the part that's in your ear, which is the speaker end. Therefore, answer A.
label: majority
